commonIssues:
Frequent session timeouts, OTP failures, and "try again" errors, particularly when adding witnesses or uploading documents.

How to fix:

  • Clear browser cache or try a different browser. Follow the guidelines on the Kaveri Online Services Portal carefully.

Strict requirements for photo sizing (max 2 MB PNG) and rejected photos (requiring formal couple photos rather than wedding attire in some cases).

How to fix:

  • Resize images to under 2MB in PNG/JPEG format. Upload clear, formal wedding photographs showing the rituals.

Variable interpretation of required documents (e.g., proof of marriage, address proof) by different Sub-Registrar Offices (SROs).

How to fix:

  • Carry multiple proofs of address and marriage (invitation card, hall receipt, photos) to the SRO to prevent rejection.

Finding three witnesses available to accompany the couple to the SRO.

How to fix:

  • Pre-arrange 3 reliable witnesses with valid Karnataka address proofs.

Under the Special Marriage Act, missing publication deadlines or providing insufficient notice.

How to fix:

  • Under the Special Marriage Act, submit the notice 30 days in advance. Follow up physically if no update is visible.

Although legally mandated for one-day issuance, applications can take weeks to get approved by SRO staff.

How to fix:

  • Visit the SRO in person with the Kaveri portal acknowledgment receipt to expedite processing.

Applications often remain pending for extended periods without updates on the Kaveri portal.

How to fix:

  • Often means manual verification is pending. Visit the registrar's office.

Unofficial demands for documentation or "fees" to hasten the approval process.

How to fix:

  • Carry all original documents uploaded online. Do not entertain unofficial fees; demand official receipts.

Typographical errors in the generated certificate requiring re-application or manual corrections.

How to fix:

  • File an amendment request at the SRO immediately with supporting correct documents.
